title: Jet Lag Clock
tagline: Get over jet lag faster with timed light exposure, backed by circadian science.
intro: This calculator tells you when to seek and avoid bright light to adjust to a new time zone faster. All calculations are performed in your browser — no data is stored or sent anywhere.
label_wake_time: What time do you normally wake up?
label_route: Where are you travelling from and to?
status_same: The origin and destination are in the same time zone. No adaptation needed — enjoy your trip!
status_adapted: Your body clock should be fully adapted by now. Have a great trip!
status_advance_day0: Your body clock is {0} hours behind local time. At ~{1} hours/day, full adaptation takes roughly {2} days. Follow the schedule below to get on track.
status_advance_dayN: Day {0} — your body clock is ~{1} hours behind local time. At ~{2} hours/day, full adaptation will take roughly {3} more days. Keep it up!
status_delay_day0: Your body clock is {0} hours ahead of local time. At ~{1} hours/day, full adaptation takes roughly {2} days. Follow the schedule below to get on track.
status_delay_dayN: Day {0} — your body clock is ~{1} hours ahead of local time. At ~{2} hours/day, full adaptation will take roughly {3} more days. Keep it up!
status_delay_around_day0: Your destination is {0}h east. Rather than advancing {0}h, it’s easier to delay {1}h — going the long way around. At ~{2} hours/day, this takes roughly {3} days.
status_delay_around_dayN: Day {0} — {1}h of delay remaining (out of {2}h). At ~{3} hours/day, roughly {4} more days to go. Keep it up!
status_switch_to_delay: Most people find it easier to delay {0}h instead.
inst_daytime_light_detail: Get bright daylight throughout your waking hours.
inst_wind_down_detail: Dim indoor lights and limit screens before bed.
inst_block_light_detail: Wear amber/blue-blocking glasses outdoors, keep lights dim inside. Morning light before your body clock’s low point would push it the wrong way.
inst_seek_light_advance_detail: Get outside into bright daylight. This is your main advance window — the more light the better.
inst_seek_light_all_day_advance_detail: Your body clock low point falls outside waking hours, so all daytime light advances your clock. Get outside freely.
inst_seek_light_delay_detail: Get outside freely. Bright light before your body clock’s low point delays it — exactly what you want.
inst_seek_light_all_day_delay_detail: Your body clock low point falls outside waking hours — all daytime light delays your clock. Get outside freely.
inst_avoid_and_wind_down_detail: Dim lights and wear blue-blockers. Light after your body clock’s low point would shift it the wrong way.
inst_wind_down_advance_detail: Dim all lights and limit screens. Evening light would push your clock the wrong way.
inst_melatonin_detail: Consider 0.5–3 mg about 30 minutes before your target sleep time. Available over the counter in some countries; requires a prescription in others.
inst_skip_melatonin_detail: Melatonin advances the clock — the wrong direction for a delay strategy.
inst_exercise_caffeine_detail: Exercise during your light-seeking window reinforces the shift. Cut caffeine by {0} (~9 h before bed).
